QF.id: III-C Prevention of Money Laundering Act_34

question.text: In the FIU Delhi vs PayPal case, the total fine imposed on PayPal was Rs. 96 lakh. If this was imposed at the maximum per-failure penalty of Rs. 1 lakh under Section 13(2)(d) of PMLA, this represents a minimum of how many individual failures?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At a maximum penalty of Rs. 1 lakh per failure, Rs. 96 lakh would represent at least 96 individual failures (96,00,000 / 1,00,000 = 96). **The Trap:** The actual number of failures could be higher if some were penalized at less than Rs. 1 lakh per failure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The large number of failures reflects the extended period of non-compliance and the FIU's characterization of the violations as 'deliberate and wilful'.
